ES1! · CME · DUAL ETH + RTH · BUILT TUE JUN 23, 2026 17:46 ET POST-CLOSE · PLAN FOR THE OVERNIGHT & WED JUN 24 RTH

ES Plan — ETH Overnight + Wed Jun 24 RTH (Yields/AI Rotation Pullback)

Dual-plan build · RTH close 7,434.75 below value, VA shift Lower (3rd down session) · Catalyst: BofA up-to-3-hikes note + historic Korean memory-stock slide drove a tech/semis rout (Nvidia, Micron, TSMC, SMH); 10-yr ~4.495% but yields fell into the close; Barclays/Stifel raised year-end S&P targets to 7,800 · VIX 19.40 flat-to-down (orderly) · Calendar: Micron earnings Wed PM, PCE Thu
RTH close 7,434.75 · ON last 7,437.25
Today (Jun 23) RTH: O 7,429.50 · H 7,491.00 · L 7,415.25 · POC 7,452.00
Today VAH 7,470.75 · VAL 7,436.25 · Poor Low 7,415.25
vs 20-EMA 7,509 −74 (LOST)
vs 50-EMA 7,401 +34 (last support)
VIX 19.40 ▼0.5% (orderly)
ATR 103.75
▼ POST-CLOSE BUILD — trend-day-down closed below value; tomorrow is "stretched, not broken" Jun 23 was a trend-day-down: ES gapped under value at the open (7,429.50), popped to a 7,491.75 selling tail, then distributed lower to a poor low 7,415.25 and closed below its value area at 7,434.75 — the third straight lower value area. The daily 20-EMA (7,509) is lost and price now leans on the 50-EMA (7,401), the last support before clean air to the 100-EMA (7,248) / prev POC (7,357). BUT three things cap the bear case: VIX fell on a −1.3% day (orderly rotation, not fear), Treasury yields eased into the close as money rotated to bonds, and sell-side raised targets to 7,800 — this reads as an AI/semis rotation pullback inside an uptrend, not a regime break. Plan: sell rips into 7,470 VAH, buy poor-low/50-EMA failures (7,415 / 7,401), and only respect a real downtrend if a fresh catalyst (Asia memory follow-through / pre-PCE) closes price below 7,415 with VIX finally expanding. Gap rule applied: the below-value close means a gap-down raises continuation odds and a gap-up raises repair odds — but the overnight coiled tight (7,421–7,452), so a near-value open (R1/R2) is the base path. MOC magnitudes noted, side unweighted per request.

01Top-Down — Overnight (Jun 23 → 24 ETH)

Scope: the Globex session from the 18:00 ET reopen to the 09:30 cash open. Reference 7,437.25. The overnight is balancing inside Today's RTH value (7,436–7,470) directly on the Jun 23 below-value close — a coil that will hand a directional read to the cash open. ETH reads against Today's profile (overnight value will be swapped in on the morning update). Same chart evidence as RTH, re-cast for the overnight.

(a) Daily — MA stack, drawn levels, narrative

ES1! Daily · close 7,443.25 · 20-EMA 7,509 / 50-EMA 7,401 / 100-EMA 7,248 / 200-EMA 7,047 / 300-EMA 6,880 · ATR 103.75 · drawn: High 7,694.75, 7,508.99, 7,401.14, 7,247.85, 7,046.93, Low 6,415.75
MAValueDist from 7,437.25Overnight relevance
20-EMA7,509.00−72Lost — overhead supply if ETH bounces
50-EMA7,401.25+36HOLDING The overnight downside backstop
100-EMA7,247.75+189HOLDING Rising trend support, far below
200-EMA7,047.00+390HOLDING Long-term trend intact
300-EMA6,880.25+557HOLDING Deep floor
Why the daily frame matters overnightFor ETH the daily read is a bracket: the lost 20-EMA (7,509) caps any overnight repair, the 50-EMA (7,401) catches any overnight flush. With the stack still bullishly stacked (20>50>100>200) and ATR 103.75 elevated, overnight ranges are wide but the structural trend is intact — so an overnight probe toward 7,401 is a dip-buy locator, not a breakdown, until a session actually closes below it.

(b) 30-min (RTH + ETH) — overnight flow, drawn levels, narrative

Block (ET)HighLowCharacterRead
Jun 23 RTH (recap)7,491.007,415.25Trend-day-down, closed below valueSets the overnight bias bearish-but-stretched
18:00–22:00 reopen~7,452~7,430Tight balance on the closeCoil building inside Today value
Asia (memory-stock window)watchwatchKey catalyst window7,415 break vs 7,452 reclaim is the tell
EuropewatchwatchOften sets cash-open leanLift to 7,470 = repair; fade = continuation

Drawn / 20-EMA cluster7,508.25black+71Hard ceiling, won't see it without a catalyst
Drawn ≈ Today VAH7,469.50black+32Overnight rally cap

Current / dotted7,437.25bluerefON last (inside Today value)

Green zone (low)7,415.25green−22Jun 23 poor low — overnight magnet
fib extend (red)7,396.50red−41≈ 50-EMA; overnight flush target
Why the overnight coil is a decision box, not a trendA tight balance (7,421–7,452) sitting on top of a trend-day-down close is a continuation-vs-reversal coil. The overnight hasn't picked a side — it has rebuilt acceptance right where the cash session left off. That means the overnight job is narrow: watch whether Asia sells 7,415 (continuation, needs a memory-stock headline) or Europe reclaims 7,452 (repair). Pressing size inside the coil is poor expectancy; the edge is at the 7,415 and 7,452 edges.

(c) Market Profile — Today's profile (overnight reads against this)

ES1! 30-min TPO Market Profile (full labels) · Today close 7,434.75 BELOW value, VA Lower · VAH 7,470.75 / POC 7,452.00 / VAL 7,436.25 · Buying Tail 7,428.75 · Selling Tail 7,491.75 · Poor Low 7,415.25 · yPOC 7,538.50 / yPoor Low 7,527.25 · prev Spike Base 7,496.25 / Single Print 7,514.25
MetricYesterday (Jun 22)Today (Jun 23)
Open7,573.257,429.50
High7,599.257,491.00
Low7,527.257,415.25
Closed7,540.257,434.75
VAH7,554.257,470.75
VAL7,527.257,436.25
POC7,538.507,452.00
Close LocInside VA > POCBelow VA
VA ShiftOverlap LowerLower

Shape & features (Today, the overnight reference). Today printed a thin p-/trend-down profile: open 7,429.50, pop to the 7,491.75 selling tail, distribution to a poor low 7,415.25, close below value. Labeled features: Today VAH 7,470.75, POC 7,452.00, VAL 7,436.25, Buying Tail 7,428.75, Selling Tail 7,491.75. The overnight is balancing in the lower half of this value.

Why Today's profile governs the overnightUntil the overnight builds its own value area, the only acceptance structure that exists is Today's. The overnight balancing in the lower half of Today value (below POC 7,452) says sellers still have the marginal edge into the reopen. The two pivots that flip the overnight read: reclaim 7,452 POC overnight = repair tell (VA trying to shift back up); lose 7,428 buying tail = the poor low 7,415 is in play. The 7,428 buying tail is the one bull artifact in the structure — it marks where responsive buyers defended into the close.

OVERNIGHT STRUCTURE MAP  (ref 7,437.25, vs Today profile)
  7,508–7,514 ── 20-EMA / drawn ceiling ............ catalyst-only
  7,470.75 ───── Today VAH ......................... overnight rally cap
  7,452.00 ───── Today POC ......................... reclaim = repair tell
- 7,437.25 - - - REF (lower half of Today value) - - -
  7,436.25 ───── Today VAL
  7,428.75 ───── Today Buying Tail ................. bull pivot (lose = poor low risk)
  7,421.25 ───── ON balance low edge
  7,415.25 ───── POOR LOW ........................... overnight magnet
  .... VOID 7,415→7,396 ....
  7,396.50 ───── fib / ≈50-EMA ..................... overnight flush target

(d) VIX & macro (overnight)

VIX 19.40, −0.5% on the day, flat-to-lower on a −1.3% S&P session — orderly repricing, not fear. Overnight macro to watch: Asian semiconductor/memory follow-through after the historic Korean slide that drove Jun 23, the aftermath of the US 2-yr auction, and positioning into Micron earnings (Wed PM) and PCE (Thu). US 10-yr yields eased into the cash close as money rotated to bonds — if that persists overnight it supports the repair case.

Why a flat VIX caps overnight downsideIf a genuine risk-off cascade were brewing you'd see VIX bid overnight; instead it eased on the down day. Falling VIX = options market pricing orderly sector rotation (AI/semis out, defensives/small-caps in), which argues against an overnight trend-leg lower absent a fresh Asia catalyst. It caps how bearish the overnight scenarios should lean.

Overnight pivots. The whole overnight story is the 7,415–7,452 box: reclaim 7,452 POC = repair tell into the cash open; lose 7,428 buying tail = the 7,415 poor low magnet activates with a fib void to 7,396 beneath it. Everything above 7,470 needs a catalyst overnight.

03Context Summary — overnight

ES1! 30-min RTH+ETH · Today close 7,434.75 BELOW value, VA Lower · all drawn levels: Today VAH 7,470.75 / POC 7,452.00 / VAL 7,436.25 · Buying Tail 7,428.75 · Selling Tail 7,491.75 · yPOC 7,538.50 / yVAH 7,554.25 · prev Spike Base 7,496.25 · fib 7,396.50 / 7,366.25
DimensionReading
Prior RTH day typeTrend-day-down — gapped under value, distributed lower, closed below VA
Settlement locationBelow value (7,434.75 vs VAL 7,436.25) — weakest close type
Overnight characterTight balance 7,421–7,452, lower half of Today value (coil)
Value migrationLower 3 sessions running (7,538 → 7,452 POC)
Ref vs Today POC/VALInside value, just above VAL, −15 from POC
Daily MA contextBelow 20-EMA (−72), above 50-EMA (+36)
ATR103.75 — overnight ranges wide
VIX19.40 flat-to-down (orderly)
Overnight catalystsAsia memory follow-through, yields, pre-Micron (Wed PM) / pre-PCE (Thu)
Why the overnight context is "coiled bearish-but-stretched"The bearish items (below-value close, VA Lower, lost 20-EMA) are short-term and already priced into where the overnight is sitting; the bullish items (rising 50/100/200 stack, flat VIX, yields easing into the close, only −15 from POC) are structural. An overnight that simply holds the coil hands a near-value open to the cash session. A genuine overnight breakdown requires a fresh Asia catalyst, which is exactly why the overnight bear scenarios are capped below the bull/neutral ones.

04Overnight Scenarios — Jun 23→24 ETH (E1–E6)

E1
Coil holds → drift to 7,452 POC (mean-revert)
TWO-SIDED1.5–2R▶
Zone
Balance 7,428–7,452 holds through Asia
Context
No fresh catalyst; overnight rotates inside the coil and gravitates to the POC magnet. Orderly VIX favors this.

Confirm
Rejections at 7,428, range contraction
Invalidate
Acceptance below 7,428 buying tail

E2

LONG2R▶
Zone
Reclaim 7,452 → push to 7,470
Context
Yields easing into the US close + soft Asia → a European bid reclaims POC and fills toward Today VAH.
Setup
Long 7,452–7,455 on a POC reclaim-and-hold · stop 7,440 · T1 7,470 · trim (VAH = overnight cap)
Confirm
30-min ON close above 7,452
Invalidate
Failure back below 7,436 VAL
Probability
★★★ 22%
E3
Asia sells the 7,415 poor low (continuation)
SHORT2–3R▶
Zone
Break below 7,415 on a memory-stock headline
Context
A fresh Asian semiconductor leg lower extends the below-value close; the void 7,415→7,396 fills fast.
Setup
Short 7,412–7,408 on a 7,415 break-retest · stop 7,429 · T1 7,396 · T2 7,366
Confirm
ON close below 7,415 + Asian semis red
Invalidate
Reclaim 7,428 (look-below-fail → E5)
Probability
★★★ 16%
E4
Overnight squeeze to 7,496/7,508 (catalyst repair)
LONG2.5R▶
Zone
Acceptance above 7,470 → vacuum to 7,496/7,508
Context
A dovish surprise (yields drop, Asia stabilizes, semis bounce) fills the overnight single-print void up to the hard ceiling.
Setup
Long pullback to 7,470–7,472 after acceptance · stop 7,455 · T1 7,496 · T2 7,508 (trim — ceiling)
Confirm
ON close above 7,470 VAH
Invalidate
Rejection back below 7,452
Probability
★★ 12%
E5
Look-below-and-fail at 7,415 (reversion long)
LONG3R▶
Zone
Probe of 7,415 that can't extend
Context
Overnight stop-run below the poor low that immediately reclaims — the poor low was already pre-sold, so a failed break traps shorts.
Setup
Long 7,416–7,422 on a 30-min reclaim of 7,428 · stop 7,407 · T1 7,452 · T2 7,470
Confirm
Wick below 7,415, close back above 7,428
Invalidate
ON close below 7,415 → flips to E3
Probability
★★ 10%
E6
Deep flush to 50-EMA 7,401 and reclaim
LONG3R+▶
Zone
Capitulation to 7,396–7,401
Context
If 7,415 breaks but the fib/50-EMA confluence holds overnight, that is the highest-quality long into the cash open — the structural trend line.
Setup
Long 7,398–7,403 reversal + reclaim 7,415 · stop 7,388 · carry to cash open · T1 7,436 · T2 7,452
Confirm
Reversal candle at 50-EMA, VIX not breaking out
Invalidate
ON close below 7,396 → stand aside
Probability
★★ 6%

05Overnight Probability Matrix

RankScenarioBiasProb
1E1 — Coil holds → drift to POCNeutral34%
2E2 — Europe lifts → VAH (repair)Bull22%
3E3 — Asia sells poor low (continuation)Bear16%
4E4 — Squeeze to 7,496/7,508Bull12%
5E5 — Look-below-fail at 7,415Bull10%
6E6 — 50-EMA flush-and-holdBull6%
CombinedBull 50% · Neutral 34% · Bear 16%100%
Overnight base case (neutral-to-slight-bull, 50/34/16). Each weight defended:
• Neutral 34% (E1) is the single most likely path because the overnight has already coiled tightly on the close — absent a catalyst, balance continues and POC pulls price.
• Bull 50% combined (E2/E4/E5/E6) rests on the orderly VIX, yields easing into the close, the rising MA stack, and the fact price is only −15 from POC — overnight probes of 7,415 are dip-buy locators, not breakdowns.
• Bear 16% (E3) is capped because an overnight breakdown requires a fresh Asia memory-stock catalyst; gravity alone rarely breaks a poor low the prior session already pre-sold, and a flat VIX argues against an overnight fear cascade.
Falsifier: Asian semis gapping down hard + an ON close below 7,415 flips this to bear-base (E3 to ~35%); a yields drop + ON acceptance above 7,470 flips it bull-repair (E4 to ~30%).

06Overnight Decision Map

GLOBEX REOPEN 18:00 ET (ref 7,437.25, vs Today profile)
│
├─ HOLDS 7,428 buying tail ───────────────► coil intact
│     ├─ reclaims 7,452 POC ──────► repair → 7,470 VAH  [E2]
│     │     └─ accepts > 7,470 ───► squeeze → 7,496/7,508  [E4]
│     └─ rotates 7,428–7,452 ─────► drift to POC, near-value cash open  [E1]
│
├─ LOSES 7,428, tests 7,415 poor low ─────► decision
│     ├─ holds 7,415 & reclaims 7,428 ───► look-below-fail long → 7,452  [E5]
│     └─ ON CLOSE < 7,415 (Asia catalyst) ► continuation → 7,396  [E3]
│
└─ FLUSHES 7,401 50-EMA & reclaims ───────► deep dip-buy, carry to open  [E6]
Why 7,428 and 7,452 are the only two levels that matter overnightThe overnight is a binary around the coil: 7,428 buying tail is the floor of the balance (lose it and the poor low magnet activates), and 7,452 POC is the repair trigger (reclaim it and the VA tries to shift back up). Everything else — 7,470, 7,496, 7,508 above; 7,396, 7,366 below — is a consequence of which of those two breaks first. Mark them and let the overnight resolve.
LevelIf it HOLDSIf it BREAKS
7,452 POCCap; rotate back to VAL (E1)Repair underway → 7,470 (E2/E4)
7,428 Buying TailCoil floor defended (E1)Poor low 7,415 in play
7,415 Poor LowLook-below-fail long (E5)Void to 7,396 (E3)
7,401 50-EMATrend intact; deep buy (E6)Regime risk → stand aside

07Overnight Bias Flip Conditions

Starting biasFlip toTrigger levelAction
Neutral coil (base)Bull repairON reclaim + hold of 7,452 POCLong into cash; target 7,470
Bull repairBull squeezeON acceptance > 7,470 VAHFill single-print void → 7,496/7,508
Neutral coilBear continuationON close < 7,415 on an Asia catalystShort retests → 7,396; prep cash gap-down
Bear continuationBull reversionReclaim 7,428 after a 7,415 probeLook-below-fail long → 7,452
AnyRegime risk (flat)ON close < 7,396/7,401 (50-EMA)Cut size; no dip-buys until reclaim

#1
POC reclaim long (E1/E2)
LONG2R▶
Entry
Long 7,438–7,442 on a reclaim/hold of 7,436 VAL with eyes on 7,452 POC

7,427 — below buying tail · risk ~11 pts

½ at 7,452 POC · ½ at 7,470 VAH (overnight cap)
Targets / R:R
T1 +14 · T2 +32 (≈2R)

Loss of 7,428 buying tail → flat
Size
Quarter size — thin overnight liquidity, ATR 103.75

Scaling
⅓ 7,436 · ⅓ 7,452 · ⅓ runner 7,470
Targets / R:R
T1 +18 · T2 +34 · T3 +52 (≈3R)

ON close below 7,415 → flat, flip to E3
Size
Quarter size; cleanest overnight long IF it triggers
#3
Catalyst continuation short (E3)
SHORT2–3R▶
Entry
Short 7,408–7,412 ONLY on a 7,415 break-retest with Asian semis red

7,429 — back into the coil · risk 17–21 pts
Scaling
½ 7,396 · trail rest toward 7,366
Targets / R:R
T1 +16 · T2 +46 (≈2.5R)
Kill switch
Reclaim 7,428 → flat (look-below-fail)
Size
Catalyst-gated; no headline = no trade
Overnight sizing. Thin liquidity + tight coil + ATR 103.75 + event cluster (Micron Wed PM, PCE Thu) = quarter size or flat. ETH trades are for positioning into the cash open, not the main event. One setup at a time; no pyramiding into the 7,415→7,396 void.

09Overnight Structural Warnings

Asia memory-stock follow-through (the catalyst risk). The historic Korean memory/chip slide that drove Jun 23 can extend in the Asian session. A fresh leg lower in semis is the single most likely trigger to break 7,415 overnight and pre-set a cash gap-down.
Poor low 7,415.25 + void to 7,396 (no-trade air pocket). Flat, tail-less base; auctions revisit it. Thin volume between 7,415 and the fib/50-EMA means overnight prints travel fast — trade the edges (7,415, 7,401), never rest limits mid-void.
Overhead single-print / spike-base supply 7,496–7,514. On a squeeze these fill quickly but that band is where overnight rallies die. Use 7,496/7,508 as trim/short zones, not breakout-chase longs unless an ON 30-min closes above.
Thin-liquidity gap risk. Overnight prints can spike through levels and reverse on low volume. Confirmed reclaims/break-retests only; respect that ATR 103.75 makes overnight ranges wide.
Event clustering. Micron earnings Wed after the bell and PCE Thursday — overnight positioning ahead of these adds two-way risk. Keep size minimal into the events.

10Overnight → Cash-Open Handoff (gap rule preview)

Gap rule. Jun 23 closed below value, VA Lower (a directional signal). A gap down at the cash open (same direction) raises continuation odds; a gap up (against it) raises repair/fill odds. Gap size judged vs ATR 103.75 (~0.3×ATR ≈ 31 pts = noise). The overnight resolution below sets which RTH scenario you walk in expecting.
Where overnight resolvesCash-open implicationWalk-in RTH expectation
Reclaims/holds 7,452 POCCash opens near-value/up (gap-up vs close signal → repair)R2/R3 repair path
Balances 7,428–7,452Cash opens inside value (no gap)R1/R2 base path
Drifts to 7,415–7,428Cash opens below VAL, inside rangeR1 look-below-and-fail
Breaks 7,415 on a catalystCash gaps down (with close signal → continuation)R5 trend-day-down II prep
Flushes 7,401 & reclaimsCash opens off a 50-EMA holdR6 deep dip-buy
Why this handoff is the whole point of the ETH planThe overnight's expectancy isn't in the trades — it's in the information. Whichever side of 7,415/7,452 the overnight closes on is your first read for the cash-open gap rule. Mark both levels before 09:30; the overnight tells you whether to arrive expecting a repair (R2/R3), a balance (R1/R2), or a continuation (R5).

11Overnight Final Summary

ItemRead
Overnight biasNeutral-to-slight-bull (50/34/16) — coiled bearish-but-stretched
Base caseCoil 7,428–7,452 holds → drift to POC into a near-value cash open
Primary tradeE1 long 7,438–7,442 on a VAL reclaim → 7,452 POC (quarter size)
Best-location alternateE5 long 7,416–7,422 on a 7,415 look-below-and-fail → 7,452/7,470
Real shortE3 short 7,408–7,412 ONLY on a 7,415 break with Asian semis red
Bull pivot7,452 POC reclaim = repair tell
Bear pivot7,415 poor low break (needs Asia catalyst) → void to 7,396
SizingQuarter size / flat — expectancy is in the cash session
Biggest riskAsian memory-stock follow-through breaking 7,415 overnight → cash gap-down
HandoffWhichever side of 7,415/7,452 the overnight closes = first read for the RTH gap rule
One-line overnight thesisCoiled on the close: watch 7,415 and 7,452, trade light, and let the overnight tell you which RTH scenario to walk in expecting.

01Top-Down Analysis — Wed Jun 24 RTH (post-close build)

Reference 7,434.75 (Jun 23 RTH close) / ON last 7,437.25. Jun 23 was a trend-day-down: ES gapped under value at the open (7,429.50) and never recovered it, closing below the value area with value shifting Lower — the third straight down session. Price sits between the daily 50-EMA (7,401) and the lost 20-EMA (7,509), with a fresh poor/weak structure overhead and an unfilled overnight void below.

(a) Daily — MA stack, drawn levels, narrative

ES1! Daily · close 7,443.25 · 20-EMA 7,509 / 50-EMA 7,401 / 100-EMA 7,248 / 200-EMA 7,047 / 300-EMA 6,880 · ATR 103.75 · drawn: High 7,694.75, 7,508.99, 7,401.14, 7,247.85, 7,046.93, Low 6,415.75
MAValueDist from 7,434.75Status
20-EMA7,509.00−74LOST now overhead resistance
50-EMA7,401.25+34Last support standing — the line in the sand
100-EMA7,247.75+187HOLDING Rising; major value-trend support
200-EMA7,047.00+388HOLDING Long-term trend intact and rising
300-EMA6,880.25+554HOLDING Deep trend floor
Why the 50-EMA is now the whole ballgameThe 20-EMA (7,509) was the buy-the-dip line for the entire April–June advance and it broke decisively two sessions ago; it is now the ceiling trapped longs sell into. That hands control to the 50-EMA at 7,401, which Jun 23 probed (daily low 7,443.25 on the futures contract, cash dug closer) and held into the close. Hold it on a closing basis and the larger uptrend is just stretched, not broken — this stays a deep pullback that mean-reverts toward 7,500+. Lose it on a 30-min/daily close and the next real shelf is the 100-EMA near 7,248, ~190 pts lower, with the prior-RTH POC 7,357.75 the only structure in between. That is the single most important either/or on the board.

Daily narrative. The stack is still bullishly sequenced (20>50>100>200>300) but price has fallen below the 20-EMA and is leaning on the 50-EMA — the first genuine character change since the April low. ATR 103.75 is elevated and expanding, so ranges are wide; position size must come down. The drawn 7,508.99 level (old support) now caps rallies, and 7,401.14 coincides with the 50-EMA to form the must-hold band. There is clean air down to 7,247.85 if 7,401 fails.

(b) 30-min (RTH + ETH) — session flow, drawn levels, narrative

SessionHighLowCloseCharacterValue migration
Jun 22 RTH7,599.257,527.257,540.25Distribution / lower-high toppingOverlap Lower
Jun 22→23 ON~7,5997,415.257,430Yields/AI flush — straight downSharply lower
Jun 23 RTH7,491.007,415.257,434.75Trend-day-down; gapped under value, no recoveryLower (below VA close)
Jun 23→24 ON (so far)~7,452~7,4307,437.25Tight balance at the lows; coilingFlat / inside Today VA

Drawn7,508.25black+74= daily 7,508.99 / 20-EMA confluence
Drawn7,482.25black+48ON supply
Drawn7,469.50black+35≈ Today VAH 7,470.75

Current / dotted7,437.25bluerefON last
Drawn7,421.25black−14ON balance low edge
Green zone (low)7,415.25green−20Jun 23 RTH/ON low (poor)
fib extend (dn)7,396.50red−38First downside fib ≈ 50-EMA
Drawn (teal)7,366.25teal−69Measured-move target
fib extend (dn)7,350.00red−85≈ prev RTH POC 7,357.75
Why the 7,415 poor low is a magnet AND a trapJun 23 ended on a poor low at 7,415.25 — a flat, unfinished base with no excess tail, built as price ran out of session rather than out of sellers. Auctions revisit poor lows to "finish" them, so 7,415 is a downside magnet: expect it to be probed. But because it was made in a hurry into the close, a *first* revisit that holds is a classic look-below-and-fail long trigger — the sellers who needed lower prices already got them overnight and into the close, so a clean break needs *fresh* supply (another yields/AI headline), not just gravity. Break-and-hold below 7,415 with conviction opens 7,396 (fib/50-EMA) → 7,366 → 7,357 prev POC.

30-min narrative. The overnight has coiled into a tight balance (7,421–7,452) directly on top of the Jun 23 close — a textbook continuation-vs-reversal coil. The structure overhead is heavy: Today VAH 7,470.75, the 7,482 supply, then the 7,508/20-EMA wall. Below, 7,421/7,415 is thin support over a fib air pocket to 7,396. The reference 7,437.25 sits inside Today's value (7,436–7,470), so the open's relationship to 7,436 (Today VAL) and 7,452 (Today POC) is the first read of the session.

(c) Market Profile — exact data, shape, features, structure map

ES1! 30-min TPO Market Profile (full labels) · Today close 7,434.75 BELOW value, VA Lower · VAH 7,470.75 / POC 7,452.00 / VAL 7,436.25 · Buying Tail 7,428.75 · Selling Tail 7,491.75 · Poor Low 7,415.25 · yPOC 7,538.50 / yPoor Low 7,527.25 · prev Spike Base 7,496.25 / Single Print 7,514.25
MetricYesterday (Jun 22)Today (Jun 23)
Open7,573.257,429.50
High7,599.257,491.00
Low7,527.257,415.25
Closed7,540.257,434.75
VAH7,554.257,470.75
VAL7,527.257,436.25
POC7,538.507,452.00
Close LocInside VA > POCBelow VA
VA ShiftOverlap LowerLower

Shape & features. Today printed a thin, elongated p-/trend-down profile: open at 7,429.50, a brief pop to 7,491 (Today RTH Selling Tail 7,491.75), then distribution down to a poor low 7,415.25 and a close below value. Labeled features carried on the chart: Today RTH VAH 7,470.75, POC 7,452.00, VAL 7,436.25, Buying Tail 7,428.75, Selling Tail 7,491.75. Above sit yesterday's relics — Yesterday RTH Poor Low 7,527.25, POC 7,538.50, VAH 7,554.25, Single Print 7,570.75, Weak High 7,599.25 — and below, Previous RTH Single Print 7,514.25/7,403.50, Spike Base 7,496.25, VAH 7,409.50, POC 7,357.75, Buying Tail 7,331.50.

Why "close below value, VA Lower" is the controlling tellThree days running the value area has migrated lower and today price closed beneath its own value area. That is the profile signature of initiative selling in control — buyers were responsive (showed up only at the lows, the 7,428 buying tail) while sellers were initiative (pressed all day). Until price closes a session back inside a value area, the path of least resistance is sideways-to-down and rallies are sell-the-rip locations, not dip buys. The 7,428.75 buying tail is the one bull artifact: it marks where responsive buyers finally defended, so it is the pivot — lose it and the poor low 7,415 is next; reclaim 7,452 POC and the auction is trying to repair.
STRUCTURE MAP  (ref 7,437.25)
  7,508–7,514 ───── 20-EMA / drawn / prev single print ........ HARD CEILING
  7,470.75 ──────── Today VAH ............................... rally cap
  7,452.00 ──────── Today POC ............................... magnet / pivot
- - 7,437.25 - - -  REF (inside Today value) - - - - - - - - - -
  7,436.25 ──────── Today VAL ............................... value floor
  7,428.75 ──────── Today Buying Tail ....................... bull pivot
  7,421.25 ──────── ON balance low edge
  7,415.25 ──────── POOR LOW ............................... magnet (finish it?)
  ........ VOID ........ thin air 7,415→7,396 ........
  7,396.50 ──────── fib / ≈50-EMA ........................... first catch
  7,357.75 ──────── Previous RTH POC ........................ deep target

(d) VIX & macro

VIX 19.40, last −0.5% on the day (O 19.45 / H 19.47 / L 19.40). The VIX is flat-to-lower on a −1.3% S&P day — a meaningful divergence. It popped from the ~17.85 base early June but has NOT broken out; on today's decline it actually eased. That is the signature of an orderly repricing / rotation, not a fear cascade. Macro: a Bank of America note flagging up to three rate hikes this year hit a tech tape already weak from a historic South Korean memory-stock slide; Nvidia, Micron, TSMC and SMH fell hard. But Treasury yields fell into the close as money rotated into bonds, and Barclays/Stifel raised year-end S&P targets to 7,800 — i.e. sell-side still treats this as a pullback in an uptrend, not a top.

Why the flat VIX caps the bear caseIf this were a genuine risk-off break you would expect VIX to be ripping on a 1.3% down day; instead it is lower. Falling VIX on a down tape = options market pricing orderly, sector-rotation selling (AI/semis out, defensives/small-caps in) rather than systemic stress. That argues against a runaway trend-day-down tomorrow and in favor of stabilization / two-sided chop once the gap is digested — it caps how bearish the probability matrix should lean even though the profile and close-location are bearish.

02Key Levels — unified ladder (ref 7,437.25)

ZonePriceSourceTypeColorDist★
R77,565.00Drawn fib / old pivotResistancered+128★★
R67,554.25yVAHResistanceblue+117★★
R57,538.50yPOCResistancegold+101★★★
R47,527.25yVAL / yPoor LowResistanceblack+90★★★
R3 🔗7,508–7,514Daily 7,508.99 + 20-EMA 7,509 + prev single print 7,514.25Triple confluence ceilingblk/red+72★★★★★
R27,496.25Previous RTH Spike BaseResistanceblack+59★★★
R1b7,491.75Today RTH Selling TailResistancered+55★★
R1 🔗7,469–7,471Today VAH 7,470.75 + drawn 7,469.50First rally capblue+33★★★★
P+7,452.00Today POCPivot / magnetgold+15★★★★
REF 🔗7,448.50ON balance shelf / fib 7,448 (green zone)Value shelfgreen+11★★★
—7,437.25ON lastReferenceblueref—
S1 🔗7,428–7,436Today VAL 7,436.25 + Buying Tail 7,428.75Value floor / bull pivotred/blue−6★★★★★
S27,421.25ON balance low edgeSupportblack−16★★★
S3 🔗7,415.25Jun 23 RTH/ON Poor Low (green zone)Poor low magnetgreen−22★★★★★
S4 🔗7,396–7,401fib extend 7,396.50 + 50-EMA 7,401.25 + drawn 7,401.14Must-hold floorred−40★★★★★
S57,366.25Drawn teal / measured moveSupportteal−71★★★
S6 🔗7,350–7,358fib extend 7,350 + Previous RTH POC 7,357.75Deep targetgold−84★★★★
S77,331.50Previous RTH Buying TailSupportblue−103★★
Controlling clusters. Two five-star magnets bracket the reference: S1 7,428–7,436 (Today VAL + buying tail — the bull/bear pivot) just below, and R3 7,508–7,514 (20-EMA + daily + single print) as the hard ceiling. Between them, R1 7,469–7,471 (Today VAH) is the first rally cap and S3 7,415 the poor-low magnet. The whole session is the fight between S1 holding (range/repair) and S1 breaking (poor-low fill → S4 7,396–7,401).

03Context Summary

ES1! 30-min RTH+ETH · Today close 7,434.75 BELOW value, VA Lower · all drawn levels: Today VAH 7,470.75 / POC 7,452.00 / VAL 7,436.25 · Buying Tail 7,428.75 · Selling Tail 7,491.75 · yPOC 7,538.50 / yVAH 7,554.25 · prev Spike Base 7,496.25 · fib 7,396.50 / 7,366.25
DimensionReading
Prior day typeTrend-day-down — gapped under value, distributed lower all session, closed below VA
Settlement locationBelow value (close 7,434.75 vs VAL 7,436.25) — weakest close type
Profile shapeThin elongated p-/trend profile; selling tail 7,491.75 up top, buying tail 7,428.75 at base
Value migrationLower — 3rd consecutive lower value area (7,538 → 7,452 POC)
Price vs POC/VAH/VALRef 7,437.25 inside Today value, just above VAL, ~15 below POC
Distance from POC−15 from Today POC; −101 from yPOC (mild extension below, room to mean-revert up)
Daily MA contextBelow 20-EMA (−74), above 50-EMA (+34) — leaning on last support
ATR103.75 (Daily) / intraday wide — size down
Catalyst regimeYields + AI/semis rotation; VIX flat-to-down (orderly), yields fell into close, sell-side raised targets to 7,800
Internals / MOCJun 23 closing imbalance net (S&P 2,373.9M / NDX 981.0M / Dow 373.7M); magnitude only, side not weighted per request
Why context = "stretched, not broken"Every bearish item (below-value close, lower value, lost 20-EMA) is an intraday/short-term signal; every bullish item (rising 50/100/200 stack, flat VIX, falling yields into the close, raised sell-side targets, only −15 from POC) is a trend/structural signal. That split is the definition of a pullback inside an uptrend: the burden of proof is on the bears to force a daily close below the 50-EMA (7,401). Until then, treat downside as locating dip-buys near 7,401/7,415 and rallies into 7,470/7,508 as faddy.

04What-If Scenarios — Wed Jun 24 RTH (R1–R6)

R1
Look-below-and-fail at the poor low (reversion long)
LONG2.5–3R▶
Zone
Open near/just below 7,415–7,428
Context
Poor low 7,415 gets probed early to "finish" it; overnight already pre-sold this area, so a flush that can't extend traps shorts.
Setup
Long 7,416–7,422 on a 30-min reclaim of 7,428 buying tail · stop 7,407 (below the void edge) · T1 7,452 POC · T2 7,470 VAH · T3 7,496 spike base
Confirm
30-min holds 7,415 then closes back above 7,436 VAL
Invalidate
30-min CLOSE below 7,415 → flips to R5 continuation
Probability
★★★★ 30%
R2
Inside-value balance / chop around POC
TWO-SIDED1.5R▶
Zone
Open 7,436–7,452 (inside Today value)
Context
Overnight already balanced 7,421–7,452; an open inside value = no edge, rotate VAL↔VAH around POC.
Setup
Fade edges: long 7,438 / short 7,468, both back to 7,452 POC · tight 9–11 pt stops · scalp only
Confirm
Rejection wicks at VAL/VAH, declining range
Invalidate
30-min close outside 7,415 or 7,471
Probability
★★★★ 26%
R3
Gap-up recovery → VAH/single-print fill (repair long)
LONG2–3R▶
Zone
Gap-up open above 7,452, into 7,470+
Context
If yields stay soft and Asia stabilizes, ES gaps back toward value. Reclaiming POC opens a vacuum fill of overnight single prints up to the 7,496/7,508 ceiling.
Setup
Long pullback to 7,452–7,455 after a POC reclaim · stop 7,438 · T1 7,470 VAH · T2 7,496 · T3 7,508 (trim — hard ceiling)
Confirm
Open above 7,452 that holds first 30-min; VA shift back to Higher
Invalidate
Failure back below 7,436 VAL
Probability
★★★ 18%
R4
Sell-the-rip into 7,470 VAH (fade the bounce)
SHORT2R▶
Zone
Bounce into 7,469–7,491
Context
Below-value close means rallies are exits for trapped longs; the Today VAH + selling tail is where responsive sellers re-engage.
Setup
Short 7,470–7,478 on a failed 30-min push (lower high) · stop 7,497 (above spike base) · T1 7,452 · T2 7,436 · T3 7,421
Confirm
30-min rejection candle at VAH, no acceptance above
Invalidate
30-min close above 7,496 spike base
Probability
★★★ 14%
R5
Gap-down / poor-low break → continuation down (trend-day-down II)
SHORT3–4R▶
Zone
Open/break below 7,415 on a fresh headline
Context
A gap down that extends the below-value close = continuation per the gap rule. The void 7,415→7,396 fills fast; only a fresh yields/AI catalyst justifies it.
Setup
Short 7,412–7,408 on a confirmed 30-min break-and-retest of 7,415 · stop 7,429 · T1 7,396 · T2 7,366 · T3 7,357 prev POC
Confirm
30-min CLOSE below 7,415 with range expansion + VIX finally rising
Invalidate
Reclaim 7,428 buying tail (look-below-fail → R1)
Probability
★★★ 9%
R6
50-EMA flush-and-hold (deep dip-buy)
LONG3R+▶
Zone
Capitulation to 7,396–7,401
Context
If 7,415 breaks but the fib/50-EMA confluence 7,396–7,401 holds, that is the highest-quality long of the week — the structural trend line.
Setup
Long 7,398–7,403 on a reversal candle + reclaim of 7,415 · stop 7,388 · T1 7,436 · T2 7,452 · T3 7,470
Confirm
30-min hammer/reclaim at the 50-EMA; VIX not breaking out
Invalidate
Daily close below 7,396 → regime shift, stand aside
Probability
★★★ 3%

05Probability Matrix

RankScenarioBiasProb
1R1 — Look-below-and-fail at poor low (long)Bull30%
2R2 — Inside-value balance / chopNeutral26%
3R3 — Gap-up POC reclaim → repair longBull18%
4R4 — Sell-the-rip into 7,470 VAHBear14%
5R5 — Gap-down poor-low break → continuationBear9%
6R6 — 50-EMA flush-and-hold (deep buy)Bull3%
CombinedBull 51% · Neutral 26% · Bear 23%100%
Base case (slight bull / two-sided, ~51/26/23). The lean is modestly constructive, and here is exactly why each input pushes the number:
• Bull 51% rests on four trend signals: the 50-EMA (7,401) held into the close and the 100/200 stack is rising; VIX fell on a −1.3% day (orderly rotation, not fear — this alone caps the bear case); 10-yr yields fell into the close as money rotated to bonds; and price is only −15 from Today POC, i.e. barely extended, with sell-side raising targets to 7,800. In that regime, probes of 7,415 are dip-buy locators (R1/R6), which is why the two long scenarios carry the most weight.
• Bear 23% is real but capped because the bearish evidence is all short-term: close below value, VA shifted Lower three days running, lost 20-EMA. That earns R4 (sell-the-rip, 14%) but NOT a high-odds trend-day-down — for R5 to fire you need a fresh catalyst (another AI/semis or yields headline) AND a 30-min close below 7,415 AND VIX finally breaking out. Absent that, gravity alone rarely breaks a poor low that overnight already pre-sold.
• Neutral 26% reflects the overnight coil: price closed the session and spent the whole overnight balanced 7,421–7,452, so an open inside that range simply continues the balance.
Falsifier: a 30-min close below 7,415 with a rising VIX and a yields up-spike would flip this to bear-base (R5 to ~35%); a 30-min acceptance above 7,470 VAH flips it to bull-repair (R3 to ~35%).

06Decision Map

OPEN (Wed Jun 24, 09:30 ET)  — GAP RULE FIRST
│
├─ GAP-UP / open ABOVE 7,452 POC ──────────► repair attempt
│     ├─ holds 30-min above 7,452 ──► LONG pullbacks → 7,470 VAH → 7,496 → 7,508 (trim, hard ceiling)  [R3]
│     └─ fails back below 7,452 ────► fade → 7,436 VAL; chop  [R2]
│
├─ OPEN INSIDE value 7,436–7,452 ──────────► balance / no edge
│     ├─ rotate up to 7,470 ───────► SELL-THE-RIP fade → 7,452 → 7,436  [R4]
│     └─ rotate down to 7,436 ─────► responsive long → 7,452 POC  [R2]
│
├─ OPEN BELOW VAL, INSIDE 7,415–7,436 ─────► look-below risk
│     ├─ probes 7,415 & holds ─────► LONG reclaim of 7,428 → 7,452 → 7,470  [R1]  ◄ highest-odds long
│     └─ accepts below 7,428 ──────► drift to 7,415 magnet, watch the break
│
└─ GAP-DOWN / break BELOW 7,415 (needs fresh catalyst) ► continuation
      ├─ 30-min CLOSE < 7,415 + VIX rising ─► SHORT retest → 7,396 → 7,366 → 7,357  [R5]
      └─ flush 7,396–7,401 & reclaims 7,415 ► DEEP DIP-BUY at 50-EMA → 7,436 → 7,452  [R6]
Why the gap branch leads the treeJun 23 closed below value with VA Lower — a directional close-location signal. Per the gap rule, a gap down (same direction as that signal) raises continuation odds, while a gap up (against it) raises repair/fill odds. But the overnight already balanced tightly on the close, so the most likely open is inside/near value, which is why R1/R2 carry the weight and the true gap scenarios (R3 up, R5 down) sit on the wings pending a catalyst.
LevelIf it HOLDSIf it BREAKS
7,470 Today VAHRally cap — fade back to POC (R4)Acceptance → vacuum to 7,496/7,508 (R3)
7,452 Today POCMagnet pin; two-sided around it (R2)Above = repair bias; below = drift to VAL
7,436 Today VALValue floor; responsive longs (R2)Exposes 7,428 buying tail then 7,415
7,428 Buying TailBull pivot defended → R1 longPoor low 7,415 in play
7,415 Poor LowLook-below-fail → strongest long (R1)Void to 7,396; continuation (R5)
7,401 50-EMATrend intact; deep dip-buy (R6)Regime shift → 7,366/7,357, stand aside

07Bias Flip Conditions

Starting biasFlip toTrigger levelAction
Neutral / slight-bull (base)Bull repair30-min acceptance > 7,470 VAHLong pullbacks to 7,452; target 7,496/7,508
Slight-bullBear intraday30-min CLOSE < 7,415 poor low + VIX risingStop dip-buying; short retests → 7,396/7,366
Bear intradayBull reversionReclaim 7,428 buying tail after a 7,415 probeLook-below-fail long → 7,452/7,470
AnyRegime shift (stand aside)Daily CLOSE < 7,396/7,401 (50-EMA + fib)Trend in question; cut size, no dip-buys until reclaim
AnyFull bull / dip over30-min CLOSE > 7,508–7,514 (20-EMA reclaim)Resume the April–June dip-buy template

08Tactical Playbook — top 3 setups

#1
Poor-low look-below-and-fail long (R1)
LONG2.5–3R▶
Entry
Long 7,416–7,422 on a 30-min probe of the 7,415 poor low that reclaims the 7,428 buying tail (retest-and-hold preferred over catching the knife)
Stop logic
7,407 — below the 7,415→7,396 void edge · risk 9–15 pts
Scaling
⅓ at 7,452 POC · ⅓ at 7,470 VAH · ⅓ runner to 7,496 spike base
Targets / R:R
T1 +30 (2R) · T2 +48 (3R) · T3 +75
Kill switch
30-min CLOSE below 7,415 → flat, flip bias to R5
Size
ATR 103.75 → size for a ~15-pt stop = ≤ ½ normal; this is the cleanest long on the board
#2
Sell-the-rip into 7,470 VAH (R4)
SHORT2R▶
Entry
Short 7,470–7,478 on a failed 30-min push into the Today VAH / selling-tail band (lower high, no acceptance)
Stop logic
7,497 — above the 7,496 spike base · risk 19–27 pts
Scaling
½ at 7,452 POC · ½ at 7,436 VAL; trail rest toward 7,421
Targets / R:R
T1 +18 · T2 +34 (≈1.8R) · runner 7,421
Kill switch
30-min CLOSE above 7,496 spike base → flat (repair under way)
Size
Counter-trend-of-the-bounce but with-trend-of-the-day; ≤ ½ normal, wider stop demands it
#3
Gap-up POC-reclaim repair long (R3)
LONG2–3R▶
Entry
Long 7,452–7,456 on a first-30-min hold above the Today POC after a gap-up open (only if yields soft + Asia stabilizes)
Stop logic
7,438 — back below VAL · risk 14–18 pts
Scaling
⅓ at 7,470 · ⅓ at 7,496 · ⅓ trim into 7,508 (hard ceiling — do not be greedy)
Targets / R:R
T1 +18 · T2 +44 (≈3R) · T3 +56
Kill switch
Loss of 7,436 VAL → flat (failed repair)
Size
Only valid on a confirmed VA-shift-back-Higher; otherwise skip
Position size vs ATR. Daily ATR is 103.75 and intraday ranges are wide. Stops here are 9–27 pts; size each trade so a full stop is a fixed % of capital, which means ≤ ½ your normal contract count this week. Do not pyramid into the poor-low void. One A-setup at a time.

09Structural Warnings

Poor low 7,415.25 (repair magnet). Flat, tail-less base from a hurried close — auctions revisit poor lows to finish them. Expect a probe; a hold = strong long (R1), a 30-min CLOSE through = continuation (R5). Don't be long through it without the reclaim.
Structural void 7,415 → 7,396 (no-trade air pocket). Thin volume between the poor low and the fib/50-EMA. Price travels fast here — no resting limits inside it; trade the edges (7,415 and 7,401), not the middle.
Spike base 7,496.25 + single print 7,514.25 (overhead supply / speed zone). The overnight selloff left single prints; on a bounce these fill quickly up to the 7,508 ceiling, but that is also where rallies die. Use 7,496/7,508 as trim/short zones, not breakout-chase longs unless a 30-min closes above.
Extension & 50-EMA proximity. Price is only −15 from Today POC (mild) but −74 below the 20-EMA and just +34 above the 50-EMA. A daily CLOSE below 7,401/7,396 is the regime line — treat it as a hard "reduce risk / no dip-buys" trigger, not just another level.
Event & gap risk. Thursday PCE and tonight/AM Asia (Korean memory-stock follow-through) are live; a fresh AI/semis or yields headline is the catalyst R5 needs. Overnight gaps are larger with ATR expanded — respect the open-print read before committing.

10Session Transition — open location → likely day type

Gap rule applied. Jun 23 closed below value, VA Lower (a directional close-location signal). A gap in that same direction (down) raises continuation odds; a gap against it (up) raises repair/fill odds. Gap size is judged vs ATR 103.75 — under ~0.3×ATR (~31 pts) treat as a near-value open.
Open vs prior RTH value/rangeGap classImplicationLikely day type
Open > 7,470 VAH (gap up, against close signal)Gap-up vs rangeFill/repair; reclaim of value, fade-risk into 7,496/7,508Repair / recovery day → R3
Open 7,452–7,470 (above POC, inside Today VA)Near-value upMild repair; POC is the pivotBalance, slight-up → R2/R3
Open 7,436–7,452 (inside Today value)Inside valueNo edge; rotate POC↔VAL/VAHBalance / range day → R2
Open 7,415–7,436 (below VAL, inside range)Look-belowProbe of poor low likely; reclaim = longLook-below-and-fail → R1
Open < 7,415 (gap down, with close signal) small <0.5×ATRGap-down, fillablePoor-low break; watch 7,396 catch & reclaimTrend-down then dip-buy → R5/R6
Open < 7,401 (gap down >0.5×ATR + fresh catalyst)Gap-and-go downOpen-drive risk; gap-fill odds drop, hold of open = tellTrend-day-down II → R5
Why an inside/near-value open is most likelyThe overnight didn't gap — it balanced tightly (7,421–7,452) right on the close, rebuilding acceptance. That means absent an Asia/PCE shock the cash session most likely opens inside Today's value, making R1/R2 the base path and reserving the true gap rows (top and bottom) for a catalyst-driven open.

11Final Summary

ItemRead
Macro biasBullish — 50/100/200 stack rising, sell-side targets up to 7,800, yields fell into close
Intermediate biasNeutral / pullback — below 20-EMA, 3 lower value areas; burden on bears to lose the 50-EMA
Intraday biasSlight bull / two-sided (51/26/23) — dip-buy locators over directional shorts
Base caseOpen inside/near value → probe of 7,415 poor low that fails → reversion long to 7,452/7,470
Primary tradeR1 long 7,416–7,422 on a poor-low look-below-and-fail (reclaim 7,428) → 7,452 → 7,470
Best-location alternateR6 deep dip-buy 7,398–7,403 at the 50-EMA/fib if 7,415 breaks but 7,396 holds
Real shortR4 sell-the-rip 7,470–7,478 into Today VAH; or R5 only on a 30-min close < 7,415 + rising VIX
Key bull level7,415 poor low / 7,401 50-EMA — hold = trend intact; 7,470 VAH reclaim = repair on
Key bear level7,415 (30-min close below) → void to 7,396 → 7,357
Biggest riskA fresh AI/semis or yields headline (Asia memory follow-through / pre-PCE) breaking 7,415 with VIX finally expanding → trend-day-down II
Day-type expectationBalance / look-below-and-fail most likely; trend-down only on a catalyst
One-line thesisStretched, not broken: sell rips into 7,470, buy the poor-low/50-EMA failures — and only respect a real downtrend if a fresh catalyst closes price below 7,415 with VIX waking up.
